nodes.label: AI as wording assistant for documents and emails
nodes.grounding_span: I've been using AI to help me get the wording correct

nodes.label: AI as brainstorming and writing tool
nodes.grounding_span: I often use AI for brainstorming tasks and writing tasks

nodes.label: AI language naturalness and authenticity
nodes.label_negative: AI language feeling forced or cheesy
nodes.grounding_span: the language feels too forced, like the model is trying too hard to be 'cool'

nodes.label: AI integration friction due to system boundaries
nodes.label_negative: Seamless AI integration within existing tools
nodes.grounding_span: it feels tricker to utilize an outside AI for some of those tasks because it lives inside its own Notion-world

nodes.label: AI involvement in value-driven core work
nodes.label_negative: AI involvement in value-driven core work
nodes.grounding_span: it feels strange to bring AI in. I want the curriculum to have heart and personality behind it

nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner vs independent delegator
nodes.grounding_span: most of my task flow with AI is more collaborative. I've not given it a ton of independent tasks

nodes.label: AI output detectability and authenticity
nodes.label_negative: AI output being obvious and detectable
nodes.grounding_span: I've tried to be really intentional to utilize AI without making it seem like I'm using AI

nodes.label: AI naming suggestions quality
nodes.label_negative: AI naming suggestions being cheesy and unhelpful
nodes.grounding_span: the suggestions always feel rather cheesy

nodes.label: AI threat to job satisfaction from repetitive tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI preserving or enhancing job satisfaction
nodes.grounding_span: it can be scary to think of not getting to do those things in the future

nodes.label: AI delegation of disliked tasks to focus on liked tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI not used for disliked tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I'd love to learn more about how to use AI to take some of the 'boring' tasks off of my plate

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: I want the time-benefits out utilizing AI

nodes.label: Authenticity and personal touch in work
nodes.grounding_span: I want the curriculum to have heart and personality behind it

nodes.label: Professional competence and skill maintenance
nodes.grounding_span: I feel competent enough to not use any sort of AI for it

nodes.label: Job security and sense of success
nodes.grounding_span: makes me not only nervous for my finances, but also for my feelings of success

nodes.label: Focus on meaningful work
nodes.grounding_span: so that I can focus on the things that I do like about my job

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for wording tasks
nodes.grounding_span: Generally, when utilizing AI for my job, I feel pretty satisfied

nodes.label: Frustration with AI naming suggestions
nodes.grounding_span: the naming suggestions are rather unhelpful

nodes.label: Anxiety about AI replacing job tasks
nodes.grounding_span: it can be scary to think of not getting to do those things in the future

nodes.label: Aspiration to delegate boring tasks to AI
nodes.grounding_span: I'd love to learn more about how to use AI to take some of the 'boring' tasks off of my plate

nodes.label: Iterative refinement through specific prompting
nodes.grounding_span: I try to be really specific in what I'm asking for, but it'll sometimes take a couple of rounds

nodes.label: Intentional concealment of AI use to maintain authenticity
nodes.grounding_span: I've tried to be really intentional to utilize AI without making it seem like I'm using AI
